text.title: Claude Agent Integration with PM Tools
text.summary: Portable agent patterns for interacting with PM tool APIs, WBS generation prompt, Jira issue creation script, and critical API gotchas.

rule.statement: Use the agent-as-scribe pattern: agent reads meeting transcripts or project briefs and creates structured issues via REST/GraphQL API. This pattern is portable across all PM tools and does not depend on vendor AI features.
rule.rationale: Vendor AI features (Jira Rovo, ClickUp Brain) are not portable; agent-as-scribe using standard APIs works regardless of PM tool vendor or pricing tier changes.

rule.statement: Always use a sandbox project for agent-generated issues before committing to the production board; require human approval before creating issues in production.
rule.rationale: Agent-generated issue quality varies with brief quality; sandbox review catches incorrect categorization, missing acceptance criteria, or wrong priority assignments before they pollute the production backlog.

rule.statement: Jira description field requires Atlassian Document Format (ADF) JSON, not plain text or Markdown — agents must generate ADF, not plain strings, for rich descriptions.
rule.rationale: Passing plain text to the Jira description field stores it as an unformatted blob without paragraphs or lists; ADF is required for formatted content.

rule.statement: Monday.com GraphQL mutations require column IDs, not column names — agents must fetch the board schema before creating items to resolve column IDs.
rule.rationale: Column names can be renamed by users; column IDs are stable. Using names causes silent failures when columns are renamed.

rule.statement: Implement exponential backoff with delays when bulk-creating issues across any PM tool API — rate limits are hit quickly during WBS creation (20–50 issues in rapid succession).
rule.rationale: All PM tool APIs enforce rate limits (Jira: 10 req/sec, ClickUp: 100 req/min, Linear: 1500 req/10min); bulk creation without delays triggers 429 errors mid-run.

section.title: Jira Issue Creation Script
section.reference: Bash script to create Jira issues from a JSON WBS output

example.code: #!/bin/bash # Create Jira issues from JSON work breakdown # Requires: JIRA_URL, JIRA_TOKEN, JIRA_PROJECT_KEY env vars create_jira_issues() { local issues_json="$1" echo "$issues_json" | jq -c '.[]' | while IFS= read -r issue; do title=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.title') desc=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.description') curl -s -X POST "$JIRA_URL/rest/api/3/issue" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $JIRA_TOKEN" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d "{\"fields\":{\"project\":{\"key\":\"$JIRA_PROJECT_KEY\"}, \"summary\":\"$title\", \"description\":{\"type\":\"doc\",\"version\":1, \"content\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"content\": [{\"text\":\"$desc\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]}, \"issuetype\":{\"name\":\"Story\"}}}" sleep 0.15 # 100ms buffer within 10 req/sec Jira limit done }
example.note: ADF format required for description field; 150ms delay keeps under 10 req/sec Jira rate limit.

section.title: DORA Paradox Monitoring

rule.statement: Monitor for the AI productivity paradox: if individual issue velocity improves but deployment frequency stays flat, identify the phase where work accumulates (review, staging, QA, deploy).
rule.rationale: DORA 2025: AI tools shift bottlenecks downstream; tracking only velocity creates a misleading signal that AI tools are delivering organizational value when they are not.

section.reference: XML prompt for WBS generation from a project brief
